query: MolmoMotion complaints OR frustrating OR broken

results.title: **Second Experience with Mammotion Support
results.content:
I’m actually based in Italy, so we don’t have the FTC here. We do have consumer protection associations, but since Mammotion is a Chinese company, I’m not sure what legal recourse I have or if our consumer protection laws would apply effectively to them.

It’s frustrating because as an early supporter and beta tester, I expected better treatment. The fact that they’re completely ignoring communications and returning products in worse condition is just unacceptable, especially for a $1500 -2000 piece of equipment as you mentioned.

I’ll definitely keep everyone updated on how this develops. Hopefully, they’ll see this post and realize they need to step up their customer service game. Early supporters like us deserve better than this.

 Reply  Share 

Image 29: u/Jimc91_Pres_Elect avatar [...] Il team di supporto alla fine ha lavorato con me in modo professionale per trovare una soluzione che funzionasse per entrambe le parti. Anche se l'esperienza iniziale è stata incredibilmente frustrante, onore al merito - hanno ascoltato, hanno fatto le dovute escalation e hanno lavorato seriamente per risolvere la situazione. Un nuovo robot è in arrivo e rispedirò quello danneggiato. Spero che questo segni la fine della mia saga di riparazioni! Grazie a tutti coloro che hanno commentato e condiviso le loro esperienze.

TL;DR: Iniziato male, ma la perseveranza ha pagato. Il team di supporto si è fatto sentire quando contava.

results.title: MolmoMotion: Language-guided 3D motion forecasting | Ai2
results.content:
### Limitations and what's next

MolmoMotion is a capable model, but there are still some limitations to note. It uses eight query points per object during training—enough to forecast a useful trajectory but not enough to densely represent surface geometry. This limits the model's handling of complex deformable motion.

We think forecasting – anticipating how objects in the world will move before they move – is as fundamental to machine intelligence as perceiving what's already there. MolmoMotion is a step toward this—3D motion prediction that generalizes across object categories without per-category templates, learned from ordinary video, and the most accurate 3D motion forecaster we've measured on PointMotionBench. We expect many applications will follow in robotics, video, and beyond. [...] |  |  |

results.title: MolmoMotion predicts 3D motion trajectories from video and text ...
results.content: Researchers note current limitations, primarily the model reliance on eight query points per object during training, which restricts its capacity to densely capture complex deformable motions. Despite this, MolmoMotion establishes a new standard for generalized 3D motion forecasting without category-specific templates. Allen AI has open-sourced the model weights, training data, benchmark suite, and implementation code, providing the research community with comprehensive resources to advance predictive AI for robotics, simulation, and media generation. [...] Researchers note current limitations, primarily the model reliance on eight query points per object during training, which restricts its capacity to densely capture complex deformable motions. Despite this, MolmoMotion establishes a new standard for generalized 3D motion forecasting without category-specific templates. Allen AI has open-sourced the model weights, training data, benchmark suite, and implementation code, providing the research community with comprehensive resources to advance predictive AI for robotics, simulation, and media generation. [...] Allen AI has introduced MolmoMotion, a novel language-guided model designed for forward-looking 3D motion forecasting. While contemporary AI systems excel at retrospectively analyzing video, MolmoMotion addresses the critical need for predictive capabilities by anticipating how objects will move in three-dimensional space. Leveraging the Molmo 2 foundation model, the system accepts an RGB video frame, a set of initial 3D query points on a target object, and a natural language instruction describing the intended action. It then outputs the future spatial trajectories of those points, effectively bridging language understanding with physical motion prediction.

results.content:
for motion capture refinement. What do you think? Drop a comment below. Next story drops in an hour. Subscribe so you don't miss it. [...] 1 comments
### Transcript: [...] all aligned so language directly drives 3D pose evolution. Does the blog say if it's open weights or API only? Full open release model code and demo on the hugging face hub that helps devs prototype fast drop in natural language commands to preview body motion without hand coding trajectories. Exactly. And since it's research grade it's mainly for experimenting with multimodal reasoning pipelines combining text 3D and time. Any early read on limitations? The post says it still simplifies complex actions and lacks real scene context, so you can't yet rely on it for highfidelity animation. Makes sense, but it's a clean entry point for integrating language into motion simulators. Totally think robotics, AR avatars, or training data generation for motion capture refinement. What do you think?

results.title: Dealing with Frustration - Scott H Young
results.content:
support, this was a very frustrating problem. On a side note to the people who organize customer service lines, if I am having a problem with your product and you have put me on hold for an extremely long period of time, I am NOT interested in buying more of your products, so save the advertisements. [...] Why are frustrating problems more energy consuming than normal tasks? The answer to this is relatively simple. Because your action is not producing the results you expect, your brain naturally goes into full gear, rapidly consuming mental energy to solve the problem at hand. In this time it is very easy to run out of energy. When your energy stores are depleted this is when you become irritable, tired, stressed and sometimes even angry.

## Take Breaks [...] ## Reward Action Not Just Results

Your environment is only going to reward your results not your actions. You won’t earn a million dollars or have a great body just because you “tried hard”. Unfortunately this is exactly what causes frustrating problems in the first place. Because your actions are not producing results, your environment is not rewarding you for taking action. In this case you need to reward yourself for taking action, even if it wasn’t fruitful.
